mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer spending without triggering monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and stable CPIAUCSL yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements and earnings revision upgrades, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through wealth and multiplier effects.
failure_condition: HOUST growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns across all market conditions over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 240.0 are followed by positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth signals an economic contraction that immediately reduces equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of late-cycle observations.
